data.title: Bone Biologics prices $5M public offering with warrants; shares up 45%
data.description:
• Bone Biologics (NASDAQ:BBLG) priced a public offering of 1.25M shares (or equivalents) at $4/share.
  • The offering includes Series D and Series E warrants to purchase up to 1.25M shares each.
  • Series D warrants are immediately exercisable at $4/share and expire in 5 years.
  • Series E short-term warrants are also immediately exercisable at $4/share and expire in 18 months.
  • Gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be ~$5M.
  • The closing of the offering is anticipated around June 30, 2025.
  • BBLG shares up 45% premarket on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Shell hikes natural gas production at Norway’s Ormen Lange field
data.description:
Shell (NYSE:SHEL) and its partners said Friday have started up two subsea compression stations at the Ormen Lange natural gas field in the Norwegian Sea to help boost recovery of gas reserves, which are exported to European markets, Reuters reported.

Shell (NYSE:SHEL) said the compression stations will raise the gas recovery rate to 85% from the current 75%, adding an additional 30B-50B cm of gas.

Ormen Lange's gas, processed at Nyhamna on the west coast of Norway, is being exported to the U.K. and the European Union.

Ormen Lange is operated by Shell (SHEL), which holds a 17.8% stake in the field, with the other stakes held by Petoro at 36.5%, Equinor (EQNR) with 25.3%, Orlen Upstream Norway at 14%, and Vaar Energi with 6.3%.

Norway has been increasing its gas production since 2022 when it overtook Russia as Europe's top gas supplier.

data.title: The U.S. isn't seeking to 'decouple' from China but to 'de-risk', Bessent says
data.description:
"We don't want to decouple from China," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Friday during a media interview. He spoke after President Donald Trump announced a trade deal with China on Thursday, though the president provided few details of the deal.

Still, the U.S. needs to de-risk, he said. "China counts for 30% of manufacturing in the world, and that is not sustainable. They need to consume more," he said. "Over the next 18 to 24 months, we will de-risk and become self-sufficient," he said in an interview on Fox Business's "Mornings with Maria."

The agreement will increase rare-earth magnets flowing into the U.S. from China. "i am confident now, we, as agreed, the magnets will flow," Bessent said. Under the agreement, the U.S. has put in counter-measures against the Chinese.

"What we're seeing here is a de-escalation under President Trump's leadership," he said.

The U.S. still has a 30% tariff on Chinese goods, and China still has a 10% tariff on U.S. goods, he said. This is a centerpiece of fair trade, which the U.S. hasn't seen in 40 years, he added.

Asked whether the U.S. can trust any deal that China signs, Bessent responded with President Ronald Reagan's oft-repeated phrase of "trust, but verify."

Bessent expects trade deals with more countries will be struck in coming weeks. "A lot of countries are feeling pressure. We have countries approaching us with very good deals," he said. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick expects 10 more deals soon, he added. "I think we can have trade wrapped up by Labor Day," Bessent said.

On Thursday, though, Bloomberg reported that trade negotiations had stalled with partners like Japan, India, and the European Union.

The Treasury Secretary also spoke about an agreement he reached with G-7 allies to remove Section 899, what's known as the "revenge tax," from the proposed tax legislation. Section 899 was a potential weapon to push back on unfair taxes from other countries, he said. "Our tax sovereignty is now guaranteed." He, however, explained that Section 891, which has been on the book for years, allows the president to impose taxes to counter unfair taxes from other nations.

One of the sticking points in the Republican's tax bill is the state and local tax ("SALT") deduction cap (currently at $10K, but due to expire under the current code. "We are very close to a deal" that's going to help voters in the SALT Republicans' districts, but also fair for the American people, Bessent said.

Senate Republicans had hoped to vote on the "One Big, Beautiful Bill" on Thursday. But that timeline stretched out after the Senate parliamentarian struck down several key parts of the bill, saying they didn't meet the rules for inclusion in a reconciliation bill, which only requires a simple majority to pass instead of a 60-vote supermajority. Congress still aims to get a bill on the president's desk by July 4.

Even though Q1 GDP was revised down on Thursday, Bessent said, "I think we'll see substantial acceleration" in the second half of the year, given the three pillars of Trump's agenda — trade, taxes and deregulation.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Fed's Kashkari expects two 2025 rate cuts, first possibly in September
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Kneel Kashkari sees room for two interest-rate cuts this year, while warning the central bank should not lock into a monetary easing path as tariff effects could hit inflation in the future, according to his essay published on Friday.

His rate projections for 2025 has not changed since December, when he "was unsure if the underlying disinflationary dynamics that we saw in 2024 would continue this year." Since then, he added, economic uncertainty surged in the face of President Donald Trump's sweeping tariffs, although inflation data continued to reflect progress toward the Fed's 2% goal.

"These opposing signals have led me to maintain my outlook for two cuts over the remainder of 2025, implying a possible first cut in September, barring some surprising development before then," the essay, published on the Minneapolis Fed's website, said.

Excluding Fed governors Christopher Waller and Michelle Bowman -- who have signaled a rate reduction as soon as July -- the majority of officials who spoke this week made plain that uncertainty remains high and a July move is not being seriously considered.

“While we gather more evidence on the true tariff shock affecting the economy, I believe we should put more emphasis on the actual inflation and real economic data that we are seeing without committing to an easing policy path in case the effects of tariffs are merely delayed,” Kashkari wrote.

If there are no trade deals and levies stay high, companies might have to pass those tariff costs on to consumers, Kashkari noted, citing his discussions with business leaders.

data.title: Bristol Myers wins favorable FDA label updates for cell therapies Breyanzi and Abecma
data.description:
Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) announced that the U.S. FDA has approved several label updates for its cancer therapies, Breyanzi and Abecma, which could help the company widen access to the two CAR-T cell therapies.

The updates are related to the use of Breyanzi in lymphomas, including large B-cell lymphoma, and the use of Abecma in multiple myeloma.

Specifically, as part of the label changes, the FDA has relaxed certain patient monitoring requirements and removed REMS (Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy) programs required to administer the drugs.

“Today's FDA-approved label updates reinforce BMS’ continued efforts to collaborate across the healthcare ecosystem, with the ultimate goal of reaching more patients and democratizing access to cell therapy,” said Lynelle Hoch, BMY’s president of Cell Therapy Organization.

Following the approval, Bristol Myers (NYSE:BMY) plans to remove the REMS programs implemented across more than 150 treatment centers allowed to administer Breyanzi and Abecma.

The company also intends to rapidly expand the network of community cancer centers nationwide that can administer Breyanzi and Abecma in a bid to cut patients’ travel time to receive the therapies.

data.title: Evercore thinks Trade Desk safe from Amazon's competitive push, but Google isn't
data.description:
Shares of Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD) rose nearly 4% in premarket trading on Friday after analysts at Evercore ISI upgraded the stock to "outperform" from a previous investment rating of "in line."

The research firm cited positive sentiment for online ad demand, improvement in execution by the company, and a strong slate of product announcements to justify the rating change.

"Our checks have indicated a clear improvement in TTD execution, both on product and go-to-market. With respect to product, our contacts have noted recent evolution of Kokai UX as a clear positive," Evercore said in its June 26 research note. "One agency programmatic lead reported that TTD client service has significantly upped its game of late and drove a highly successful Solimar-to-Kokai transition in late Q1/early Q2."

On Thursday, Wells Fargo cut its rating on TTD, fearing competition from Amazon (AMZN) going ahead, but Evercore, while noting that competition is intensifying, expects Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) to be more at risk due to this.

Evercore thinks that Google's DV360 is likely to be the primary share donor to Amazon's DSP, as "AMZN’s power alleys are much more likely to overlap with DV360’s existing footprint than TTD’s."

Looking into 2026, the equity ratings firm sees multiple catalysts that should help the company to sustain growth of close to 20% on an ex-political basis. They believe that midterm elections have the potential to layer on an additional point of growth.

Catalysts for growth according to Evercore include Netflix (NFLX), growing retail media budget allocations and improvements in TTD’s Retail Sales Index targeting and attribution capabilities, the 2026 FIFA World Cup and Winter Olympics, and Kokai.

TTD's price target of $90 was maintained, implying an upside of nearly 29%.

data.title: Eastman board appoints new director
data.description:
• The Board of Directors of Eastman Chemical (NYSE:EMN) has appointed Mr. Damon Audia as a director.
  • Audia is Senior Vice President and CFO for AGCO, an $11.7B global leader in agricultural machinery and precision ag technology.
  • Prior to this, he held key financial leadership roles at Kennametal Inc., Carpenter Technology Corporation, and The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co.

data.title: PCE in charts: Core rate ticks up in May
4 stocks to watch on Friday: EL, CORZ, META, ALK
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Friday as investors looked forward to the Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation gauge. Here are four stocks to watch:

  • Estée Lauder (EL) shares gained 2.5% in extended trading as HSBC upgraded its rating to Buy from Hold and also upped its price target to $99 from $80. The change in recommendation comes a few days after Deutsche Bank raised the personal care stock to a Buy rating after having it set at Hold, citing improving fundamentals for the firm. 
  • Core Scientific (CORZ) stock rose 7.3% in premarket trade after media reports said that the hyperscaler CoreWeave (CRWV) is in talks to acquire the data center infrastructure company. Although no figures have been revealed, a transaction could be finalized in a few weeks. The development comes after last year, when CoreWeave attempted to acquire Core Scientific for about $1B, but that deal was rejected.
  • Meta (META) shares were up 0.9% in premarket trade as the European Commission said that the company may face daily fines if limited changes that the company has proposed to its pay-or-consent model do not comply with an antitrust order issued in April. META and the EU have had their fair share of tussles, with the regulator fining Meta (META) €200M for allegedly breaching the EU's Digital Markets Act, or DMA.
  • Alaska Air (ALK) shares will be under the radar after the group said it has identified a cybersecurity incident in its unit. Its unit, Hawaiian Airlines, had faced the incident, which affected certain information technology systems, on June 23. The firm said it has yet to determine if the event has materially impacted its financial condition or results of operations.

data.title: INmune Bio surges ahead of mid-stage trial readout for Alzheimer’s drug
data.description:
• Shares of INmune Bio (NASDAQ:INMB) extended after-hours gains in the pre-market trading on Friday with a ~61% rise as Wall Street reacted to a company event scheduled next week to disclose mid-stage trial data for its Alzheimer’s drug XPro.
  • The Boca Raton, Florida-headquartered biotech said it will hold a conference call on Monday, June 30, at 8.00 EDT to announce initial data from its Phase 2 MINDFuL trial for XPro in early Alzheimer’s disease.
  • An inhibitor of tumor necrosis factor (TNF), XPro, unlike currently available TNF inhibitors, neutralizes soluble TNF without any impact on transmembrane TNF or TNF receptors, helping to decrease neuroinflammation in conditions such as Alzheimer’s.
  • Seeking Alpha analyst Anders Research issued a Buy rating on INmune Bio (NASDAQ:INMB) last week, noting that the stock indicates a “high-risk, high-reward opportunity ahead of its Phase 2 Alzheimer’s readout.”
